ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#Florian.kuenne@abbott.com;]
question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#Florian.kuenne@abbott.com;]
answer:
Hi Dear,
Thanks for your message and interest in the reComputer Industrial J4012.
https://www.seeedstudio.com/reComputer-Industrial-J4012-p-5684.html

Regarding your questions:
Certifications (RoHS / REACH):
All relevant compliance and certification information (including RoHS and REACH) can be found on the product page under the Documents / Certification section. You should be able to download the certificates and declarations directly from there. These documents are published by the manufacturer and cover this specific product SKU 110110191 – reComputer Industrial J4012.
Product Lead Content:
The certification documents on the product page should include the RoHS declaration, which states compliance with the applicable hazardous substance limits. If you require a specific “lead-free / no lead at all” declaration (beyond standard RoHS compliance), please let us know the exact wording/level of detail required, and we can follow up with the manufacturer on this. (Standard RoHS compliance does not always guarantee zero lead in every component unless explicitly stated.)
Digital Outputs – Voltage and Current:
The product page shows that the unit includes 4 digital inputs (DI) and 4 digital outputs (DO) among the I/O interfaces.
However, the technical specifications on the site do not detail the voltage level (e.g., 3.3 V / 5 V / 24 V) nor the maximum current drive per output.
Could you please clarify what you mean by “digital output voltage and current”? For example:
Are you asking whether the DO pins are logic-level signals (for interfacing with controllers) or power-driving outputs that can directly drive external loads?
Do you need to know whether these outputs are configurable (adjustable) via software or hardware?
Once we have this clarification, we can get precise electrical specs from the manufacturer.
Looking forward to your reply.

question: Australia and Singapore，can show me different options for 4G and 5G On Fri, 30 Jan 2026 at 1:23 pm, Seeed TechSupport Team <techsupport@seeed.io> wrote: Dear , Thank you for your r...
question_subject: I just saw that reComputer Super J3011 has a SIM card port. Is that means we can directly insert a simcard inside to let it get the lte internet without any extra device or module
answer:
Hi , Dear ,
Thank you for your message.
To connect the reComputer Super J3011 to the internet using a SIM card, an external cellular modem module must be installed in the Mini-PCIe slot. The onboard SIM card slot is designed to work together with the LTE module and cannot provide mobile data connectivity by itself.
For your reference, we have a verified 4G LTE module test case in our official wiki documentation:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/recomputer_jetson_super_hardware_interfaces_usage/#mini-pcie
Based on our validation, we recommend using a 4G LTE Mini-PCIe module, as it is generally more stable and widely supported on Jetson platforms compared to 5G solutions.
If you share your target carrier in Australia and Singapore, we can further suggest the most suitable 4G module variant for your deployment.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#rohaid.ahmed@bcubelimited.com;]
question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#rohaid.ahmed@bcubelimited.com;]
answer:
Dear Mr. Mirza,
Thank you for reaching out and providing the details of the issue.
From your description, it appears that the Jetson AGX Xavier Development Kit is getting stuck during the boot process (black screen) when the custom FPGA card, powered by an external supply, is connected. Since the system does not complete the boot sequence, services such as SSH are also unavailable.
This behavior is commonly associated with hardware initialization problems during early boot, especially when externally powered PCIe or custom expansion devices are attached. In such cases, the Jetson platform may halt during PCIe device enumeration or power sequencing checks.
We would kindly suggest the following points for investigation:
Power Sequencing and Timing
Please ensure that the FPGA card is not powered on significantly earlier than the Jetson device. Incorrect power-up timing can cause PCIe initialization failures during boot.
Common Ground Requirement
When using an external power source for the FPGA card, it is essential that the FPGA power supply and the Jetson Development Kit share a common ground reference.
PCIe Reset and Clock Signals
Verify that the FPGA card follows the Jetson PCIe specifications, including correct handling of PERST# (reset) and reference clock requirements.
Reverse Power or Backfeeding Check
Externally powered devices may unintentionally backfeed power through PCIe pins, which can prevent the Jetson module from starting properly.
Isolation Testing
As a first step, please confirm that the Jetson Development Kit boots normally when the FPGA card is disconnected. This will help isolate the issue to the custom hardware interface.
If possible, please share additional details such as the FPGA card interface type (PCIe lane configuration), power sequencing design, and any schematic references, so we can further assist with a more targeted analysis.
We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to supporting you in resolving this issue.

answer:
Hi,
Sorry for the inconvenience.
Regarding the RTC battery: because RTC batteries are difficult for us to keep in stable inventory and to ship/stock consistently, our boards are currently shipped with the RTC battery not installed by default. In most use cases, you can simply sync the system time after connecting the device to the Internet (e.g., via NTP), so manual RTC battery installation is not strictly required unless you need accurate timekeeping while offline.

answer:
Hello,
Thanks for the update.
This largely depends on your working environment. In a typical indoor setup, our heatsink/cooling solution can generally handle the MAXN power/thermal load, so the system should operate normally.
The MAXN warning is a system-level detection message. On older Jetson developer kits, the stock cooling is often not sufficient to sustain MAXN under heavy loads, so the warning can appear more easily. If your CPU temperature remains within a normal range and you are not observing actual performance drops, you can generally ignore the warning.

ticket_subject: Hello, I have a J101 and Jetson Nano and I want to buy a heatsink for it. I was just wondering if an aluminium heatsink would be sufficient? And would you recommend installing some extra SSD memory? I want to try and run nano LLM’s on it. Best...
question: QuestionHello, I have a J101 and Jetson Nano and I want to buy a heatsink for it. I was just wondering if an aluminium heatsink would be sufficient? And would you recommend installing some extra SSD memory? I want to try and run nano LLM’s on it. Bes...
question_subject: Hello, I have a J101 and Jetson Nano and I want to buy a heatsink for it. I was just wondering if an aluminium heatsink would be sufficient? And would you recommend installing some extra SSD memory? I want to try and run nano LLM’s on it. Best...
answer:
Hello Sjoerd,
Thanks for your message.
Heatsink / cooling
An aluminium heatsink is generally sufficient for the Jetson Nano / J101 in typical use, as long as it has adequate surface area and good contact with the module (with a proper thermal pad or thermal paste).
However, if you plan to run heavier workloads (like continuous AI inference), I strongly recommend using a heatsink + active fan. With sustained high load, active airflow makes a big difference in keeping the board stable and avoiding thermal throttling.
SSD / storage
Adding an SSD is a good idea if you need:
more storage space than the microSD offers
better stability and faster read/write speeds (microSD cards can be a bottleneck and wear out faster)
That said, please note: installing an SSD improves storage capacity and speed, but it does not increase RAM.
Running nano LLMs on Jetson Nano
One important point: Jetson Nano is quite limited for running “nano LLMs” locally, mainly due to its GPU performance and (especially) RAM capacity. You may be able to run very small models with aggressive quantization and optimization, but performance may be slow and you could run into out-of-memory issues depending on the model and framework.
If your main goal is to run nano LLMs smoothly, you may want to consider a more capable Jetson platform (e.g., Orin series), but if you’d like, you can share the model size / framework you plan to use and I can suggest what’s realistic on Jetson Nano.

question: Hi, I received the order and have had no success getting the device set up. I’ve done everything I can find online but can’t get jetpack loaded. Is there way to exchange the device...
question_subject: Re: Invoice for your Seeed order
answer:
Hi, Dear
Could you please let us know the specific issue you are encountering that prevents you from entering the system?
For example, does the device fail to boot, or does the JetPack installation stop at a certain step?
If the device cannot boot normally, we recommend re-flashing the system by following our Wiki guide, especially the physical steps for entering Force Recovery Mode:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_J4012_Flash_Jetpack/#enter-force-recovery-mode
Please note that the physical operation steps for the reComputer J40 series are very similar to the official NVIDIA developer kit.
The main difference is that you need to download and use the following flashing package (which already includes CUDA and related environments):
https://seeedstudio88-my.sharepoint.com/my?id=%2Fpersonal%2Fyoujiang%5Fyu%5Fseeedstudio88%5Fonmicrosoft%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FJetson%2DImages%2Fother%2Fmfi%5Fjetson%2Dorin%2Dnano%2Ddevkit%2Dsuper%2D36%2E4%2E3%2D2025%2D11%2D21%2Etar%2Egz&parent=%2Fpersonal%2Fyoujiang%5Fyu%5Fseeedstudio88%5Fonmicrosoft%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FJetson%2DImages%2Fother&ga=1
You can use this package to re-flash the device and reset the system.
Please feel free to share more details about the issue, and we will be happy to assist you further.

ticket_subject: Inquiry: reComputer Robotics J4011 Specifications and Availability (6 USB 3.2 ports),Super Mode, and SDI Input Compatibility
question: ---------- Forwarded message --------- Šalje: miro nikoletic <miro.nikoletic@gmail.com> Date: sri, 28. sij 2026. 14:21 Subject: Inquiry: reComputer Robotics J4011 Specifications an...
question_subject: Inquiry: reComputer Robotics J4011 Specifications and Availability (6 USB 3.2 ports),Super Mode, and SDI Input Compatibility
answer:
Hi，

Yes, reComputer Robotics provides 6 × USB 3.2 Type-A (5 Gbps) ports. However, we have not tested connecting six USB LTE/5G modems simultaneously. You may certainly purchase one unit first to evaluate your specific use case.
The Orin NX module used in reComputer Robotics does support MAXN SUPER mode. The factory image is preinstalled with JetPack 6.2.
Regarding the power adapter, we recommend using a 19V or 24V power supply. The required power rating can be referenced from the power adapter we provide. In most scenarios, the supplied adapter is sufficient, but you should also check the power requirements of the USB LTE/5G modems you plan to use.

answer:
Dear Domenico,
Thank you for the detailed information.
Sorry to confirm that ODYSSEY X86J4125800 v2 (SKU 102110767) is currently out of stock / discontinued, so we’re not able to supply this model continuously.
Recommended alternatives (board-only, equal or better performance)
If you need a board without any cover/case, the closest replacement we can recommend is our ODYSSEY X86 v2 board series (11th Gen Intel® Core™), which offers higher performance than the Celeron® J4125, while keeping rich connectivity and an embedded MCU co-processor:
ODYSSEY-X86i31125G4 (Intel® Core™ i3-1125G4) – dual 2.5GbE, Windows/Linux support, and onboard Arduino co-processor ATSAMD21

ticket_subject: Odyssey x86 j104 - Can't recognize drives on NVME to Sata adapter
question: To add on to that as well, just to confirm. Would something like this likely work in the M.2 B-Key socket? From: Nolan Allaire Sent: Wednesday, January 28, 2026 2:15 PM To: techsup...
question_subject: Odyssey x86 j104 - Can't recognize drives on NVME to Sata adapter
answer:
Hi ,
Thanks for sending that over.
To answer your question: Yes, the drive in the image (TeamGroup SATA M.2) probably will work in the M.2 B-Key socket.
Here is the confirmation based on the specs:
Protocol: The drive label explicitly states "SATA3," and the Odyssey board’s B-Key socket is designed to support the SATA protocol.
Connector Type: The drive in the photo has two notches (known as a "B+M Key"). This design is specifically intended to fit into standard B-Key sockets.
Just a quick note: Be sure to use the B-Key socket for this drive. The other slot on that board (the M-Key) is generally reserved for NVMe (PCIe) drives, so this SATA drive likely wouldn't be detected in that specific slot.

ticket_subject: Hello I have a question about this product, https://www.seeedstudio.com/reComputer-Super-J4012-p-6443.html I have a few questions about the WiFi options: When I select "for Wi-Fi/BT" or "for Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE" option, does the product come with t...
question: QuestionHello I have a question about this product, https://www.seeedstudio.com/reComputer-Super-J4012-p-6443.html I have a few questions about the WiFi options: When I select "for Wi-Fi/BT" or "for Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE" option, does the product come with t...
question_subject: Hello I have a question about this product, https://www.seeedstudio.com/reComputer-Super-J4012-p-6443.html I have a few questions about the WiFi options: When I select "for Wi-Fi/BT" or "for Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE" option, does the product come with t...
answer:
Hi Toan,
Thanks for reaching out.
Regarding the “for Wi-Fi/BT” and “for Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE” selections on the reComputer Super J4012 page: these options are describing the wireless connector/interfaces supported by the carrier board (i.e., what the hardware is designed to support), rather than indicating that a Wi-Fi module is factory pre-installed. 

For what’s included in the full system package, please refer to the Part list in the official reComputer Super User Manual. The standard box contents list items such as the Jetson module, carrier board, NVMe SSD, case/heatsink, USB cable, and manual—it does not list a Wi-Fi module or antennas as included by default.
If you need a known, tested option, we recommend our wireless accessory: RTL8822CE Wireless NIC Kits (M.2 Wi-Fi + BT). This kit also includes the antenna set (2× antennas + coax cables) as shown on its product page.
https://www.seeedstudio.com/RTL8822CE-Wireless-NIC-Kits-for-Nvidia-Jetson-Orin.html
About using other Wi-Fi modules (e.g., Intel AX210NGW): we don’t claim official support/validation for every third-party module. In practice, some modules may require additional driver installation and compatibility checks on Jetson/Linux (for example, Infineon/Intel wireless solutions may involve backport driver packages and have known conflicts depending on what’s installed). ()
So if you choose a different card, you may need to install/configure the driver on your side.

ticket_subject: Clarification on USB Host Controller Architecture for J401 Carrier Board
question: Dear Seeed Studio Team, I am Sanjay Johny from Swapp Design Private Limited, and we are developing an autonomous battery swapping solution for electric 4-wheelers using AMR technol...
question_subject: Clarification on USB Host Controller Architecture for J401 Carrier Board
answer:
Dear Sanjay,

Thank you for your detailed inquiry. Below is a concise conclusion:
The reComputer J401 does not feature two or more independent xHCI USB host controllers.All USB Host ports on the J401 are derived from a single xHCI controller integrated within the Jetson Orin Nano module.
The multiple onboard USB 3.2 Type-A ports (as well as the Type-C Host port) are implemented via USB Hub expansion,rather than mapping to separate independent USB Host controllers respectively.
Therefore, despite the fact that the J401 is equipped with multiple physical USB 3.0/3.2 interfaces, all USB cameras will still share the same xHCI controller and total bandwidth. This means the device does not meet the architectural requirement of "two independent USB Host controllers, each with multiple ports of its own".

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#sanjay@swapp.design;]
question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#sanjay@swapp.design;]
answer:
Hi, dear
This board does NOT meet your requirements. Unlike the J4012, the reComputer Robotics Carrier Board uses a USB Hub architecture rather than separate Host Controllers.
Single Controller Constraint: All 4 USB 3.1 Type-A ports on this board share the bandwidth of a single xHCI Root Port (USBSS0) from the Orin module via a USB5744T Hub. They are not independent.
Bandwidth Bottleneck: Connecting 5 cameras to this board would force them to compete for the same 5Gbps/10Gbps link, which is likely to cause data loss or instability for simultaneous reading.
Missing Second Controller: The specific requirement for "minimum 2 separate xHCI USB HOST controllers" is not met by the external ports on this carrier board. (The second USB controller USBSS1 is routed to the internal Mini PCIe slot, not to external USB ports).
Recommendation: Stick to the previous reComputer Mini J4012 with extension board

https://www.seeedstudio.com/reComputer-Mini-J4012-with-Extension-p-6353.html
solution (or a similar carrier with a dedicated PCIe-to-USB chip), as that hardware architecture provides the physical isolation and dedicated bandwidth required for your 5-camera application.

Thank you for your detailed testing and analysis.
Yes, the cable length can indeed be a potential issue. A 40 cm extension cable may introduce signal attenuation, impedance mismatch, or additional capacitance, which can affect the PWM signal integrity and result in a lower peak-to-peak voltage—especially if the signal is not properly buffered.
We recommend testing with a shorter cable if possible, or using a shielded cable / signal buffer to improve signal quality. Your observation is consistent with what can happen in such scenarios.
Please let us know if you need further assistance.

ticket_subject: Request for Technical Clarification on PoE-Related Diagnosis – reComputer Industrial J3011
question: Dear Seeed Studio Technical Support Team, Greetings from ERIC Robotics. We are writing to seek technical clarification regarding an issue reported on the reComputer Industrial J301...
question_subject: Request for Technical Clarification on PoE-Related Diagnosis – reComputer Industrial J3011
answer:
Hi,

Thank you for detailing your use case and sharing your understanding of the IEEE 802.3 PoE mechanism.

We need to clarify a critical technical point here: the Ethernet port labeled "PoE" on the reComputer Industrial J3011 is a PoE PSE (Power Sourcing Equipment) port. It is designed to supply power to external devices (e.g., PoE cameras), and is not a PoE PD (Powered Device) port, nor does it support receiving power input from a PoE switch.
Therefore, directly connecting this PoE PSE port to a PoE-enabled switch port constitutes an unintended PSE-to-PSE connection. In such scenarios, even if the switch complies with the IEEE 802.3 standard and the device itself is not powered via PoE, abnormal electrical stress may still occur at the port level due to the interaction between the power supply circuits on both ends, which could lead to hardware damage.

Based on the above, the fault characteristics in this case are consistent with PoE-related electrical damage caused by non-supported usage, and are not covered by the standard PoE PD negotiation process.

To prevent similar issues from recurring, we recommend:
- Connecting the J3011 to a non-PoE switch port, or
- Disabling the PoE function on the corresponding port of the switch, and using a PoE isolation solution if necessary.

ticket_subject: Issue with reComputer Industrial J3011- Fanless Edge AI Device with Jetson Orin Nano 8GB module(476149)
question: Hello @techsupport, One of our customers has reported a critical issue observed with the Industrial Orin Nano – J3011 unit supplied by you, and we would like to escalate this matte...
question_subject: Issue with reComputer Industrial J3011- Fanless Edge AI Device with Jetson Orin Nano 8GB module(476149)
answer:
Hi,

we believe the customer has misunderstood the PoE port on the device. Please refer to the wiki for details: the PoE label on the device indicates PSE (Power Sourcing Equipment), which means the J3011 supplies power to external devices (e.g., a PoE Camera). It does not mean the J3011 itself is powered via the RJ45 port from a PoE switch.

The customer connected the device directly to a PoE switch port without using a PoE isolator/splitter. This caused PoE voltage to be applied to a non-PD (Powered Device) port, resulting in electrical damage to the hardware. We apologize, but this is classified as user-induced damage.

ticket_subject: 客户购买的 reComputer J4012 模块在首次使用时无法显示屏幕输出，尽管连接了所有必要外设并且设备电源和风扇正常工作，请求紧急支持解决问题并附上参考视频。
question: Dear Sir, We bought the reComputer J4012 module with the following details: S/N: 110110145251200057. When attempting to power on the device for initial use and testing, no output a...
question_subject: 客户购买的 reComputer J4012 模块在首次使用时无法显示屏幕输出，尽管连接了所有必要外设并且设备电源和风扇正常工作，请求紧急支持解决问题并附上参考视频。
answer:
Dear Omar,
Hello, thank you for contacting us and for providing the detailed description and video.
For the current issue, please try the following troubleshooting steps one by one:
Disconnect all external peripherals
Please unplug all external devices (keyboard, mouse, Ethernet cable, USB devices, etc.), and connect only the HDMI cable to the monitor. This helps rule out the possibility that an external peripheral is preventing the device from booting properly.
Check the boot log
You can follow the instructions in our Wiki to obtain the system boot log, which will help identify where the startup process may be failing:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/get_the_system_log_of_recomputer_j30_and_j40/
Re-flash the system image
If the issue persists, we recommend re-flashing the system by following the steps in the Wiki below:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_J4012_Flash_Jetpack/#flash-jetpack
Please let us know the results after performing these steps, or share the boot log if available, so we can further assist you in diagnosing the issue.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#styeung@lscm.hk;]
question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#styeung@lscm.hk;]
answer:
Hello,
Thank you for your message.
Regarding the issue you described on the reComputer Industrial J4012, where the system often stops at a blank screen with a cursor after the logo, we would like to confirm the following points with you:
Have you ever executed the command sudo apt upgrade (or similar system upgrade commands) on the device?
Please note that this operation may replace some of the pre-installed dependencies with NVIDIA official versions, which can overwrite our board-specific adaptations and lead to boot failures.
If there is no critical data that needs to be preserved on your development board, based on our experience, this issue is very likely to require re-flashing the system image to resolve.
You may follow the instructions in our official Wiki to re-flash JetPack:
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/reComputer_Industrial_Getting_Started/#flash-jetpack
If you encounter any difficulties during the flashing process or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us.

question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#m.alhashim@aigcom.com;]
answer:
Hi,

The issue of the GPU not being detected on your Jetson device, with only the CPU showing up, is usually not a hardware problem.
The GPU on Jetson is integrated into the SoC and does not support the nvidia-smi command, which can cause some platforms to incorrectly report "no GPU detected".

ticket_subject: reComputer Super J4012 - Jetson™ Orin™ NX 16GB Super Edge AI Device,Power Adapter 3P-Black-19V-4.74A/7.4*5mm,Power Adapter 3P-Black-12V-5A/7.4*5mm
question: Hi, I am looking into the specs of reComputer Super J4012 model. reComputer Super J4012 - Jetson™ Orin™ NX 16GB Super Edge AI Device |157 TOPS Performance I want to know which powe...
question_subject: reComputer Super J4012 - Jetson™ Orin™ NX 16GB Super Edge AI Device,Power Adapter 3P-Black-19V-4.74A/7.4*5mm,Power Adapter 3P-Black-12V-5A/7.4*5mm
answer:
Hi,

if you need the device to operate in MAXN Super Mode (157 TOPS peak performance) for a long time, or if you have multiple external devices such as CSI cameras and LTE modules connected, we recommend prioritizing the 19V-4.74A adapter (Option 1) to ensure stable power supply.
If you mainly use the device in 25W or lower power mode (e.g., regular AI inference, lightweight video analysis) and have few external devices connected, the 12V-5A adapter (Option 2) will suffice for your needs while being more energy-efficient.

question_subject: Hi, We are looking at the J501 board for possible use in a product we are designing. I have two questions: 1) In the description of the board you state that "Camera add-on expansion boards will be available to interface directly with high-de...
answer:
Hi,

Regarding “Camera add-on expansion boards will be available to interface directly with high-density MIPI CSI interfaces”:
The J501 camera expansion board is designed for GMSL cameras. Although MIPI CSI signal routing is used on the board, the solution is based on a GMSL SerDes architecture, rather than direct native MIPI CSI camera connections.
At present, GMSL camera support is limited to several specific camera models from selected vendors that we have already validated. If support for a different GMSL camera is required, additional customization (including driver and device tree adaptation) would be necessary.

Regarding “is there a hardware input that can be used to tell the module / Jetson module that a power-off is being requested (for example, when the battery level is low)”:
The J501 carrier board does not provide a dedicated hardware pin to directly cut off system power. However, a safe and controlled shutdown can be triggered in software by executing:
sudo shutdown now
This command transitions the system into a powered-off state, and is functionally equivalent to:
systemctl poweroff
From the backend perspective, this performs a graceful shutdown of the system, after which the carrier board and module stop operating.

question: Hi Lorraine, The screenshot you provided does not seem to be the same one I am referencing to. I believe I am using a Type-C cable with PD protocol, I have it hooked up to an ATZ U...
question_subject: Flashing Jetpack 6.2 Jetson Orin NX reComputer J4012
answer:
Hi Nawfal Zainuddin,

We apologize for the delayed response, as China is observing the New Year's Day holiday. We wish you a happy new year!

We have checked the product link you provided.

Currently, we do not recommend using a multi-functional Type-C Hub (such as an adapter with both PD power supply and USB expansion) on the reComputer Jetson Robotics J401. This is because on embedded platforms like Jetson, compatibility issues may occur during the negotiation of Type-C PD and DisplayPort Alt Mode. In some cases, this can result in normal USB functionality but no HDMI display output.

For better compatibility and stability, we suggest directly using a USB-C to HDMI direct cable. Please ensure the cable is clearly labeled as supporting DisplayPort Alt Mode (DP Alt Mode) and requires no driver installation. Such direct connection solutions have a higher success rate on embedded platforms.

question_subject: Created its own password can’t get past setup
answer:
Hello Víctor,
Thank you for reaching out to us.
To better assist you, could you please provide some additional information about your device? Specifically, we would like to confirm the exact model of the development board you purchased (for example, the full reComputer model name).
If you are unable to log into the system due to an unknown or auto-generated password and cannot proceed past the setup screen, we recommend reflashing the system image, which will reset the device and allow you to start fresh.
Please refer to our official wiki for detailed reflashing instructions:
Follow the steps corresponding to your specific reComputer model
After reflashing, the system will be restored to a clean default state
Once you confirm the board model, we can also provide you with the most relevant documentation link.
We look forward to your reply and will be happy to assist you further.

ticket_subject: We recently bought this reserver, we would like to connect GMSL2 cameras with fakra connectors, we don't have this ports in the actual reserver. Which would be the easiest way for us to do it? is it any aviable? Or do we need to change the reserv...
question: Technical Support
question_subject: We recently bought this reserver, we would like to connect GMSL2 cameras with fakra connectors, we don't have this ports in the actual reserver. Which would be the easiest way for us to do it? is it any aviable? Or do we need to change the reserv...
answer:
Hi,

1.There are manufacturers on the market that supply GMSL2 decoding boards (Serializer → MIPI CSI). These boards convert the high-speed serial signals of the Fakra interface into standard CSI interface signals, and you may purchase them on your own.

2.Replace the carrier board with one that supports GMSL2.
reComputer Robotics J401 carrier board of NVIDIA® Jetson™ Orin™ NX/Orin™ Nano

Could you please assist us with the above troubleshooting steps? If a hardware fault is confirmed, we will replace the item with a new one for you.

ticket_subject: CSI-2 Compatibility Question — reComputer J3011 + DS90UB954 EVM (RAW10/RAW12)
question: Good Morning, I hope this message finds you well. I have a Seeed Studio reComputer J3011. According to the datasheet, it supports a 16-pin CSI camera interface. I am using a TI DS9...
question_subject: CSI-2 Compatibility Question — reComputer J3011 + DS90UB954 EVM (RAW10/RAW12)
answer:
Hi,

I think you might have checked the wrong document. Our datasheet specifies a 15-pin CSI interface.

1. The reComputer J3011 carrier board comes with native 15-pin CSI interfaces (2 independent 2-lane MIPI CSI-2 interfaces), and there is no native 16-pin CSI physical interface.

2. RAW10/RAW12 formats are fully supported.

3. Standard CSI-2 signaling (HS/LP modes) is fully supported.

We do not provide a dedicated document for this; only the schematic diagram is available for reference.Find it under Resources on the wiki page.
Getting start with reComputer J30/40 | Seeed Studio Wiki

question_subject: Wifi dongle not recognized on reComputer Industrial J4012
answer:
Hi,

that’s correct. We have made relevant driver adaptations for JetPack 5—it comes with additional Realtek WiFi drivers pre-installed by default (such as rtl8723du, rtl8192cu, etc.).

For JetPack 6, the kernel configuration is more streamlined, so only a limited set of drivers are enabled by default, and these WiFi drivers are not included. You may refer to our wiki tutorial to compile the drivers yourself.

You will need to manually compile and install the driver based on its chip model, and you can refer to our Wiki for guidance.
https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/how_to_build_the_source_code_project_for_seeed_jetson_bsp/

Alternatively, you can purchase a USB WiFi adapter with a compatible RTL8723DU chip.

question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#hyungi5754@kookmin.ac.kr;]
answer:
Hi guy,

It looks like the GPIO pin you enabled is not the correct one — the pin you used applies to reComputer Mini, not reComputer Robotics.
Please send me the full output of gpioinfo (in terminal). From that information, we should be able to identify the correct GPIO pin.
In addition, we also need to confirm a few things:
Are you using the MyActuator motor SDK? The CAN driver for this motor can be found here: https://wiki.seeedstudio.com/myactuator_series/#build-python-and-c-environment
Are you using the correct CAN baud rate?
Have you brought up the CAN interface with:
sudo ip link set can0 up

question_subject: Re: 回复：[## 333955 ##] Request for Exchange.
answer:
Hi Justin,

Thank you for your feedback. Regarding the chassis, we would like to clarify the following: our integrated device enclosure is not available for separate retail sale. It is structurally designed to match the complete product and cannot be provided as a standalone accessory.

This is indeed a matter of product positioning. Our carrier boards are primarily targeted at customers with independent design capabilities—for instance, those who have their own chassis solutions or require customized development based on specific application scenarios. If a customer needs a plug-and-play, all-in-one protection solution, purchasing the complete device would be a more sensible option.

In response to your concern about chip overheating, we will be launching a heat sink compatible with the ReComputer Super and Robotics J401. It will be available for individual purchase starting January 2025, providing basic protection and eliminating the risk of component damage.

Practical recommendations:

1.Use the heat sink as a temporary solution: Purchase it as soon as it launches in January to address your urgent cooling needs.

2.Explore rapid customization options: Contact local 3D printing or CNC machining services, and use the 3D files we provide to produce a simple enclosure quickly. This approach offers controllable costs and lead times.

3.Evaluate the complete device package: If customization costs turn out to be too high, we recommend purchasing our complete device directly, as it will be more cost-effective in the long run.

We understand this may not be the ideal answer you were looking for, but this is the inherent positioning of our carrier board product line. Please make a decision based on your technical capabilities and budget.

question_subject: A subset of these 5V 4A power supplies from previous orders (made on a colleagues account) have failed when being used to power Jetson Nano Developer Kits. Has this behavior been recorded before or are there conditions that may lead to this outco...
answer:
Hello,

Considering the high cost of international return shipping, we currently do not recommend sending the power adapters back to us, as the shipping cost may exceed the value of the product itself.

To determine the cause of the issue more accurately, we would appreciate your assistance in confirming the usage duration of these power adapters:
- Are they old adapters that have been used for one year or longer?
- Or did the three adapters fail shortly after purchase and within one year of use?

Please note that power adapters may also experience performance degradation or damage if stored for extended periods without use.

For adapters that have been in use for a long time, we recommend replacing them with new ones directly to ensure the stability of your devices.For adapters purchased recently, we suggest observing them for a while longer to see if the same issue persists.

If you find any faulty adapters that are still within the warranty period, please feel free to collect and send them back to us. We will conduct further inspection and analysis for you.

Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have any further questions. We are always ready to assist you.

answer:
Hi,

Thank you for your consultation. Below is the overview regarding the use of this aluminum heat dissipation module under 85% humidity conditions:
Key Risks:
1. The service life of the fan may be shortened by over 50%, with a high likelihood of bearing corrosion and motor failure.

2.The oxidation rate of the heat sink will accelerate, resulting in a 5–10% reduction in heat dissipation efficiency.

3.The risk of condensation will increase significantly, which may cause short circuits in the Jetson module.

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#arcadence@gmail.com;]
question:
### 客户问题总结
1.  **设备与配置**
    服务器搭载 64GB DDR4 内存、4TB NVME 硬盘、64GB USB（用于启动 UnRAID 系统）及 Noctua NF-A9x14 PWM 风扇，使用原装交流电源线；配备两块希捷 Exos 22TB 7200RPM SATA 企业级硬盘，分别为型号 ST22000NM001E 与 ST22000NM000C。

2.  **核心故障现象**
    - 原配置为 1 块 22TB 硬盘 + 1 块 2TB 硬盘时，服务器运行无异常；更换为 2 块 22TB 硬盘后，多数情况下开机仅运行约 2 秒即自动关机。
    - 偶发能成功启动的情况，启动后重启服务器可正常运行，且双硬盘均能被识别；但客户担心断开电源后，再次开机仍会出现无法启动的问题。
    - 此前双硬盘无法识别的问题已解决，原因为第二块硬盘在 BIOS 中被禁用。

3.  **客户推测与诉求**
    - 推测故障原因可能是**电源供电不足**，因为双 22TB 硬盘的启动功耗高于原配置。
    - 提出两个诉求：一是咨询更换电源的相关方案；二是询问 BIOS 中是否有硬盘**延迟启动（错开硬盘spin up时序）** 的设置选项。

question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#aryan@bharatrohan.in;]
answer:
Hi，Dear,
Thank you for purchasing the A603 Carrier Board. regarding your questions about the USB ZIF port and power settings, please see our responses below:
1. USB 3.0 ZIF Port (W11) Connection Regarding the 1x USB 3.0 0.5mm pitch 20P ZIF port, please refer to our official technical documentation for the detailed pinout and interface definitions:
Technical Document:
Leetop A603 Carrier Board V2.1
Please note that we do not currently provide or sell a specific FPC-to-USB 3.0 converter board for this interface. We suggest searching for a generic 20-pin 0.5mm pitch FPC breakout board or converter online. You can use the pin definition in the linked PDF above to verify compatibility or to design your own custom cable.
2. Power Requirements & Super Mode Regarding the power draw and performance modes:
Important Warning on Super Mode: If you are using Orin NX 8GB or Orin NX 16GB modules with JetPack 6.2, we strictly do not recommend enabling "Super Mode" (MAXN). Enabling this mode on this specific configuration may cause significant thermal issues and overheating.
Power Supply: The 7A rating is a maximum specification to ensure stability when multiple peripherals are connected. For standard usage, the board will draw current based on the module's load and attached devices.
We hope this information helps. Please let us know if you have further questions.

ticket_subject: Issues Flashing NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB DevKit Module on Seeed Studio reComputer Mini J40 Carrier Board
question: Hello, As the subject line states, I'm running into issues with flashing an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB DevKit Module on the Seeed Studio reComputer Mini J40 Carrier Board. I was f...
question_subject: Issues Flashing NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB DevKit Module on Seeed Studio reComputer Mini J40 Carrier Board
answer:
Hi,

please troubleshoot the issue by following these steps:

During the Jetson flashing (firmware/OS installation) process, the device needs to mount a shared directory from the host PC via NFS to read the rootfs or flashing image. When Jetson uses initrd flash, a USB0 interface is used to map an NFS network drive.

1.Ensure the NFS server on the Ubuntu host is running.Use this command to check if there is status output:systemctl status nfs-kernel-server
2.Refer to the response from the NVIDIA official forum and disable the host's firewall.
https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/flash-fails-orin-agx-at-99/312585
https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/either-the-device-cannot-mount-the-nfs-server-on-the-host-or-a-flash-command-has-failed-while-using-sdk-manager/312719/3
sudo ufw status
sudo ufw disable
3.It is recommended to use a standalone Ubuntu host, rather than a virtual machine (VM) or Docker.
4.Format the NVMe SSD to the ext4 file system before use (this is a prerequisite for successful flashing).
5.You can try installing the dependency tools required for Jetson device flashing:
sudo apt install qemu-user-static sshpass abootimg nfs-kernel-server libxml2-utils binutils -y
6.If the issue persists, we recommend using the SSD sold by us (to ensure compatibility with the flashing process).
https://www.seeedstudio.com/NVMe-M-2-2280-SSD-256GB-p-5333.html

question_subject: A608 Driver for Arducam B0215 IMX-477
answer:
Hi there,

To evaluate the feasibility of adapting your company's GMSL camera to the A608 carrier board, we need your team to provide the following technical documents:

Hardware Compatibility Confirmation
- GMSL Deserializer Board Schematic: Please provide it in PDF format for us to verify the CSI interface level, power supply timing, and FPC connector definitions.
- Interface Consistency: Is the MIPI output interface (15-pin/22-pin FPC) of your deserializer board physically compatible with the A608 CSI-2 interface? If there are discrepancies, can alignment be achieved via an adapter FPC?
- Hardware Status: Please confirm that your GMSL deserializer board has passed functional verification and can stably output MIPI signals.

Software Configuration Parameters
- GMSL Link Initialization Script: Please provide the I2C register configuration sequence of the MAX9272A/MAX9296 deserializer, including parameters such as power-on timing, link rate, and address mapping.
- Camera Driver Status: Does your company have a complete driver package (device tree + kernel module) that can enable the camera on Nvidia's original development board?

Camera Specifications and ISP Support Scope
- Camera Datasheet: Please provide the complete datasheet of the IMX-715/IMX-415 sensor (including register definitions).
- ISP Configuration: Does your camera module have a built-in ISP?
Without ISP: We will only provide enabling support (image output is possible, but image quality such as color and exposure is not guaranteed).
With ISP: Please provide the ISP register configuration table or initialization script.
Please compile the above documents and send them to us. After evaluation, we will provide an adaptation solution or clarify the technical boundaries.

2025 12月 08 03:25 下午
( 回复于 2 天 )
Dear Tommaso,
Thank you very much for your detailed feedback and for sharing your findings.
Regarding the CSI bandwidth of the reComputer Robotics carrier, the total theoretical bandwidth is 20 Gbps (8 lanes × 2.5 Gbps per lane). Each GMSL camera occupies 2 CSI lanes, and for one 4K (3840×2160) @ 30 fps GMSL camera, the required bandwidth is approximately 3.89 Gbps. Therefore, from a pure bandwidth perspective, streaming four 4K GMSL cameras simultaneously should be feasible on this platform.
Based on your description—where the system becomes unstable when the third camera is enabled—we preliminarily suspect that the issue may be related to the current device tree configuration on the carrier board, rather than a fundamental CSI bandwidth limitation.
We have noted that you were able to stabilize the system by modifying the device tree (including the use of two PHY IDs on the deserializer side). At this stage, we cannot yet confirm whether this approach is optimal or fully aligned with our official configuration.
We have already escalated this case to our internal engineering team for reproduction and verification testing. Once we complete our internal validation, we will provide you with:
Our official findings on this issue
The recommended and validated configuration (if adjustments are required)
We kindly ask for your patience during this process, and we will keep you informed as soon as we have concrete results.

question_subject: [Compatibility Inquiry] 8× SG3S-ISX031C at 1080p30
answer:
Dear Mohamed Kamel,
Thank you for your patience.
Regarding the adjustment of exposure, gain, and saturation on GMSL cameras, we would like to share some important references and community discussions for your further testing and verification.
First, please note that:
Exposure and gain are sensor-level parameters and are adjustable via I2C only if the driver correctly maps the V4L2 controls to the sensor registers.
Saturation, contrast, noise reduction, and color processing are ISP-level parameters, which require the NVIDIA ISP pipeline (nvarguscamerasrc) and proper ISP tuning files. These controls will not take effect when using direct V4L2 RAW capture (v4l2src).
When the ISP is bypassed, images often appear noisy and washed out, which matches the behavior you described.
To help you better understand and test these limitations, we recommend reviewing the following Jetson community forum discussions and official documentation:
V4L2 exposure and gain control on Jetson (community discussion)
This thread explains how exposure and gain behave through v4l2-ctl, and why the settings may not take effect if the driver does not properly support them.
NVIDIA Developer Forum – IMX219 exposure/gain via V4L2:
https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/t/how-to-set-exposure-and-gain-parameters-for-imx219-with-v4l2-ctl/149092
Jetson Linux Driver Package – GMSL / SerDes limitations
The official Jetson Linux release notes describe several important constraints when using GMSL + serializer/deserializer architectures, including clocking and ISP-related limitations that may affect sensor control and image quality.
NVIDIA Camera Architecture (ISP vs V4L2 RAW path)
This documentation explains the difference between:
Direct V4L2 capture (RAW / no ISP)
NVIDIA ISP pipeline (nvarguscamerasrc)
and why ISP is required for parameters like saturation and noise reduction.
https://docs.nvidia.com/jetson/archives/r36.3/DeveloperGuide/SD/CameraDevelopment.html
Exposure & gain behavior and noise characteristics (technical background)
This paper explains why exposure and gain alone cannot fully solve noise and washed-out images without proper ISP processing:
https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.12646
Suggested Actions for Your Setup
We recommend that you try the following based on the above references:
Verify whether you are using the ISP pipeline
If you are using v4l2src, the ISP is bypassed.
Please test using:
gst-launch-1.0 nvarguscamerasrc sensor-id=0 ! nvvidconv ! autovideosink
Check whether exposure and gain controls are truly supported by the driver
v4l2-ctl -d /dev/videoX --all
Confirm whether ISP tuning files for ISX031 are present in your system
Typically located under:
/var/nvidia/nvcam/settings/
At this stage, these references should help you determine:
Whether your current pipeline is bypassing the ISP
Whether the driver correctly supports sensor-level exposure/gain
Whether missing ISP tuning is responsible for the noisy and washed-out image

ticket_subject: Hello! I can see the reComputer Robotics J401 Carrier Board for Jetson Orin Nano with the GMSL expansion board is compatible with several GMSL cameras. Have you tried using four SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A concurrently? The overall bandwidth should be f...
question: QuestionHello! I can see the reComputer Robotics J401 Carrier Board for Jetson Orin Nano with the GMSL expansion board is compatible with several GMSL cameras. Have you tried using four SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A concurrently? The overall bandwidth should ...
question_subject: Hello! I can see the reComputer Robotics J401 Carrier Board for Jetson Orin Nano with the GMSL expansion board is compatible with several GMSL cameras. Have you tried using four SG2-AR0233C-5200-G2A concurrently? The overall bandwidth should be f...
answer:
Dear Tommaso,
Thank you very much for your detailed feedback and for sharing your findings.
Regarding the CSI bandwidth of the reComputer Robotics carrier, the total theoretical bandwidth is 20 Gbps (8 lanes × 2.5 Gbps per lane). Each GMSL camera occupies 2 CSI lanes, and for one 4K (3840×2160) @ 30 fps GMSL camera, the required bandwidth is approximately 3.89 Gbps. Therefore, from a pure bandwidth perspective, streaming four 4K GMSL cameras simultaneously should be feasible on this platform.
Based on your description—where the system becomes unstable when the third camera is enabled—we preliminarily suspect that the issue may be related to the current device tree configuration on the carrier board, rather than a fundamental CSI bandwidth limitation.
We have noted that you were able to stabilize the system by modifying the device tree (including the use of two PHY IDs on the deserializer side). At this stage, we cannot yet confirm whether this approach is optimal or fully aligned with our official configuration.
We have already escalated this case to our internal engineering team for reproduction and verification testing. Once we complete our internal validation, we will provide you with:
Our official findings on this issue
The recommended and validated configuration (if adjustments are required)
We kindly ask for your patience during this process, and we will keep you informed as soon as we have concrete results.

question_subject: Re: Confirmation of Order Details & Shipping Information
answer:
Hi,

Thanks for sharing the video. Based on the information you provided, this issue is most likely caused by the following problems:

1.USB power supply limitation of reComputer Industrial (5V 2.1A; it will trigger over-current protection if exceeded). The RT5572 is an older-generation chip, and some high-power USB WiFi adapters may experience an instantaneous current surge under certain circumstances.

2.The USB 3.0 controller of the Jetson platform has poor compatibility with some USB 2.0 devices (RT5572 is a USB 2.0 device).

3.We currently only provide driver support for the RTL8822 series WiFi chips. Drivers installed by other users cannot be guaranteed to work properly.
Therefore, we recommend that if you want to use a USB WiFi module, it is best to use a USB Hub with an external power supply, and choose a module equipped with the RTL8822 WiFi chip.

In addition, we would like to confirm one more thing. As far as we know, we have a customized process of soldering the WiFi module onto the Lookout product for you. Are you currently using the standard off-the-shelf reComputer Industrial?

answer:
Hi,

1.The jumper between BRD_ID_WP and GND is DNP (Do Not Populate).
2.The BRD_ID_WP cannot be directly used to trigger the strobe light.

We recommend the following hardware operations:
1.Solder a 2-pin header at position J5 → to obtain the FRSYNC signal (1.8V).
2.Use an oscilloscope to test TP18 first and check if there is any signal present.

For the software part, you will need to configure it on your own; we currently do not provide relevant guidelines or tutorials for this.

answer:
Hi Leo,

The two headphone interfaces of the Industrial J501 separately as Line IN and Line OUT.
Line IN:
Used as an audio input interface. Headphones must not be connected for recording. It should be connected to another audio output device.
Line OUT:
Used as an audio output interface. It can be connected to headphones or speakers.
Recording test (for reference):
amixer -c APE cset name="ADMAIF1 Mux" I2S1
arecord -D hw:APE,0 -c 2 -r 48000 -f S16_LE -d 15 my.wav
Playback test (for reference):
amixer -c APE cset name="I2S1 Mux" ADMAIF1
aplay -D hw:APE,0 file_example_WAV_10MG.wav
aplay -D plughw:APE,0 file_example_WAV_10MG.wav

ticket_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#josserand-pierre.gay@cnrc-nrc.gc.ca;]
question: Dear Technical Support Team, You have received a technical support inquiry from a customer. Please respond to the customer as soon as possible using the provided customer email add...
question_subject: Technical Support Inquiry - Please Respond to the Customer [#josserand-pierre.gay@cnrc-nrc.gc.ca;]
answer:
Hi,

Thank you for your message.

On the A608 carrier board, UART1 (W16) is enabled by default and is intended for general-purpose UART communication, which is why it works without any additional configuration.

UART2 (W27), however, is not enabled by default. On the A608, UART2 is multiplexed with other functions and its availability depends on the device tree and BSP configuration, which are currently closed-source and not user-configurable.

Even if the standard device tree for Jetson Orin NX 16 GB is installed, UART2 may remain unavailable unless it is explicitly enabled in the carrier-board-specific device tree provided by Seeed. At this time, there is no additional user-side configuration or special procedure to enable UART2 beyond the supported default setup.

Therefore:

UART1 (W16): enabled and supported by default

UART2 (W27): not enabled by default, and not guaranteed to be available

If your application requires multiple UARTs, we recommend continuing to use UART1 or considering alternatives such as USB-to-UART adapters.

Please let us know if you have further questions.

ticket_subject: Can't flash a reComputer J4012 following a version update
question: Hi, so yesterday, to help my luck i partitionned my computer so i an have a ubuntu 20.04.6 lTS directly on it and not via a virtual machine. I tried this morning but now the usb ti...
question_subject: Can't flash a reComputer J4012 following a version update
answer:
Hi,

thank you for your best efforts in troubleshooting. However, we still need your assistance to diagnose the following issues:

1.  Disable USB Auto-suspend (Most Likely to Resolve the Issue)
    Ubuntu 20.04 has USB auto-suspend enabled by default, which will put the Jetson into sleep mode during the flashing process. This is the primary cause of early-timeout errors.

    # Run the following commands on the host computer
    sudo -s
    echo -1 > /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/autosuspend
    exit

2.  Force USB 2.0 Mode (Critical for USB-C Hosts)
    Your USB-C-only host is likely running on USB 3.0, but the USB-C device port of the J401 only supports USB 2.0. This incompatibility can lead to communication failure.

    Try the following methods:
    Option A - Use a USB 2.0 Hub (Recommended)
    Connect a powered USB 2.0 hub between the host and the Jetson; this will force communication at USB 2.0 speed.

    Option B - Use a USB 2.0 Port Adapter
    If you have a hub with USB-A 2.0 ports, connect via an adapter instead of using a direct USB-C connection.

3.  If you see a prompt indicating that a device is attempting to connect while checking the logs, you can try manually unplugging and re-plugging the device’s power supply. The device may then connect to the Linux host normally.

4.  Was your device functioning properly the last time you used it?

answer:

question_subject: 大卫询问如何降低其i2c端口7的速度，并询问是否有相关工具可用。
answer:
Hi,

we sincerely apologize, but we do not recommend or support this method.
The Device Tree and related low-level configurations used on this carrier board are currently closed-source. The key parameters of the I²C controller (including the clock frequency) have been hardcoded in the closed-source DTB file and no modification interfaces have been made available to the public.
Therefore, we are unable to provide the Device Tree file or relevant support for modifying the I²C bus operating frequency.
